title: pinus uliginosa var. conica
intro: Pinus uliginosa var. conica, also known as the Bog White Pine, is a conical-shaped variety of the Pinus uliginosa tree species, native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. Traditionally, the needles and seeds of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to combat infections, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The medicinal properties of Pinus uliginosa var. conica have been associated with several health benefits, including the potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's needles and seeds are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or decoctions, or extracted as essential oils to harness their medicinal properties.
